stemHtml: Which of the following medical conditions can arise due to the human body being subjected to a rapid decrease in pressure, equivalent to an altitude of 18,000 feet or higher?

options.a: The expansion of the gases inside the lungs may lead to barotrauma.
options.b: The expansion of the gases inside the lungs may lead to breathing in excess of the ventilation required to remove carbon dioxide, developing into hyperventilation.
options.c: The nitrogen dissolved in the blood may come out of solution as small bubbles, which leads to decompression sickness.
options.d: The oxygen dissolved in the blood may come out of solution as small bubbles, which leads to anaemic hypoxia.
